sentence_reviews.text: PDDL, STRIPS, and hierarchical task-network planning represent actions with preconditions and effects, then search for a valid plan from an initial state to a goal.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA classical planning, STRIPS/PDDL-style action schemas, and hierarchical planning material.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited source directly supports action preconditions/effects and planning as search from initial state to goals.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of PDDL, STRIPS & hierarchical task-network planning is to connect Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning, Discrete mathematics & logic to downstream capabilities including Formal verification of agents & plans.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA planning, logic, and formal reasoning material; formal-verification capability link is synthesized.
sentence_reviews.notes: Planning and logic links are supported; the formal-verification downstream role is plausible but not directly stated.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Formal verification of agents & plans
sentence_reviews.notes: Formal action models can support verification, but the exact downstream capability is not directly cited.

sentence_reviews.text: combined with Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning
sentence_reviews.notes: HTN planning directly combines planning with hierarchical task decomposition.

sentence_reviews.text: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning, Discrete mathematics & logic.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA planning/search complexity and logic material.
sentence_reviews.notes: Search cost and dependencies are supported; self-evaluation/confident-error wording is broader than the cited evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: PDDL, STRIPS & hierarchical task-network planning uses Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.notes: HTN planning directly depends on task decomposition; STRIPS/PDDL planning also uses goal/subgoal structure.

sentence_reviews.text: PDDL, STRIPS & hierarchical task-network planning uses Discrete mathematics & logic as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA logical action representations and planning formalisms.
sentence_reviews.notes: Preconditions, effects, states, and goals are formal/discrete logical structures.

sentence_reviews.text: PDDL, STRIPS & hierarchical task-network planning uses Graph search, BFS/DFS & A* as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA planning-as-search and heuristic search material.
sentence_reviews.notes: Planning can use graph/heuristic search, but not all PDDL/HTN planning is specifically BFS/DFS/A*.

edge_reviews.notes: Task decomposition is central for HTN and relevant to structured planning.
Planning representations directly use discrete logical state/action models.
Graph/heuristic search is a common planning method, but not mandatory for every HTN/PDDL planner.
